Tests the Boolean conversion of an empty string to false.

# Purpose
This code is a simple assertion test that checks the behavior of the `Boolean` function in JavaScript. It verifies that an empty string `""` is correctly converted to the boolean value `false`. The assertion uses the `assert` function to ensure that the expression `false === Boolean("")` evaluates to `true`. This test is useful for confirming the expected type conversion behavior of JavaScript's `Boolean` function when applied to an empty string.
